reachability_artifacts: built sworn baton diff and sworn doctor --sync-baton runs prove exact v0.16.0 parity and isolated installer output without a sibling Baton checkout

effort_complexity.rationale: A bounded but broad exact-byte vendor and installer parity upgrade across archive, schemas, mirrors, and public commands.

release_benefit: Pins every supported local Baton surface to the exact v0.16.0 protocol before release-mode behavior depends on it.

design_decisions.choice: Adopt Baton v0.16.0 through a fresh, exact vendor/archive/installer tail slice while preserving the immutable started v0.15.1 bootstrap evidence in S20.
options: append a pinned v0.16.0 parity slice after S22/S20
rewrite S20 in place from v0.15.1 to v0.16.0
treat the local Baton checkout or a real user home as installation authority
design_decisions.human_decision: Ratified by Brad (Coach), 2026-07-18: replan the active release for Baton v0.16.0, update local installations through the bounded release work, and retain started S20 lifecycle evidence rather than rewriting it.
design_decisions.rationale: An exact archive and native transaction retain clean-CI and real-home safety, while an append-only slice preserves the pre-existing v0.15.1 lifecycle and avoids fabricating replacement evidence.

positive_scenarios: A clean checkout and isolated Codex/Claude homes agree with the exact tagged v0.16.0 protocol.
negative_scenarios: Missing, corrupt, mismatched, or partial vendor/archive/installer state fails closed without mutating a real user home.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: One exact archive and transaction prevent the binary, vendored protocol, and installed agent guidance from silently diverging.
